headline: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: Rivian, Intuitive Machines, Rigetti Computing — And Dogecoin Analyst Foresees Drop To 30 Cents
summary:
Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors’ favorite stocks over the last week — here’s a look at some of our top stories.



The major indices experienced consecutive weekly losses, with the S&P 500 falling 1.9%, the Nasdaq Composite dropping 2.3%, and the Dow sliding nearly 1.9% this week.

headline: Mark Zuckerberg Critiques 'Culturally Neutered' Companies, Advocates For 'Masculine Energy' As Meta Cuts DEI Programs Post-Joe Rogan Appearance
summary: Meta co-founder and CEO Mark Zuckerberg expressed concerns about companies distancing themselves from "masculine energy" during a podcast with Joe Rogan.

headline: Jeff Bezos' Amazon To Pay $40 Million For Melania Trump Documentary: Report
summary: Amazon is reportedly investing $40 million in a documentary about Melania Trump, directed by Brett Ratner.

headline: Elon Musk's X, Hewlett Packard Strike $1 Billion AI Server Deal: Report
summary: Hewlett Packard has reportedly secured a deal worth over $1 billion to supply servers optimized for AI to Elon Musk's social media platform, X.

headline: T-Mobile Sued Over Massive Data Breach Impacting 79 Million Americans: Sensitive Information Included Names, Addresses, And More
summary: Washington state has taken legal action against T-Mobile for allegedly neglecting cybersecurity vulnerabilities, leading to a significant data breach in 2021 that impacted 79 million people across the U.S.

headline: Apple Staring At Market Share Decline In China Amid Declining iPhone Shipments: Top Analyst Flags Why Some Models Could Be Face Shipping Momentum Challenges
summary: Apple is experiencing a drop in its market share in China. This decline is attributed to a decrease in iPhone shipments, as reported by supply chain analyst Ming-Chi Kuo.

headline: 'Steve Jobs Invented The iPhone And Now They're Just...Sitting On It:' Mark Zuckerberg Hints At Apple's Potential Downfall Amid iPhone Maker's 'Random Rules'
summary: Mark Zuckerberg, the CEO of Meta (NASDAQ: META), on Friday criticized Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) for its lack of innovation and restrictive policies. He also hinted at the potential downfall of the tech giant if it doesn't adapt to the rapidly changing industry.

headline: Google TV Reportedly Struggles With Missing Movie Purchases: Disney Classics And New Releases Now Locked Behind Streaming Services (Updated)
summary: Google TV users are facing issues with purchasing certain movies, including Disney classics, as some titles are now only accessible via streaming services instead of direct purchase options.

headline: Meta Documents Reveal Secret Policy Protecting Big-Spender Advertisers From Automated Content Moderation: Report (Updated)
summary: Meta's internal documents reportedly reveal a policy that shields high-spending advertisers from automated content moderation, sparking controversy over fairness and transparency in the company's advertising practices.

headline: Mark Zuckerberg Says Biden Officials Berated Meta Staff As They Refused To Remove 'True' Content: 'It Just Got To This Point...That's Ridiculous'
summary: Zuckerberg has admitted that officials from the Biden administration have been exerting pressure on Meta staff to remove specific content.

headline: Apple CEO Tim Cook Took Home $74.6 Million In 2024, Up 18% Despite AI And Siri Privacy Controversies
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook saw a increase in his annual compensation, despite the company facing controversies over its AI, Siri privacy policies.
